figma_prototype_data: none — web-handoff file exposes no reactions/interactions/transitions via MCP. All actions/motion below are inferred from convention for the Bodhi yoga site.

tailwind_primitives.hover-underline: relative after:absolute after:left-0 after:bottom-0 after:h-px after:w-0 after:bg-current after:transition-all hover:after:w-full

motion.rationale: Card is interactive surface — subtle lift + soft shadow on hover. Tailwind-only, no JS, keeps it a server component.
Parent orchestrates child entrance stagger when the card enters the viewport.
First visual element to settle (left half hero photo).
Title is the focal point of the right column — leads the content cascade.
Feature tiles animate as a single row (FeatureTile children inherit, no individual stagger).
Price sits in the top-right column — arrives in parallel with feature row for visual balance.
Primary action lands last so the eye terminates on it.
Decorative trailing caption — last to settle, total cascade ≤ 300ms after image (image 0ms → cta 240ms → caption 300ms). With ~400ms tween duration this completes by ~700ms total.

actions.rationale: Entire card is a clickable surface leading to the course detail page (slug derived from title 'Decoding "What is Prana?"'). Bodhi convention assumed; orchestrator should confirm.
Button label 'Book spot now' → booking flow for this course. Nested inside card link; component-builder must stop event propagation so card-click and CTA-click don't both navigate.
Subtle brightness shift + active press-scale on the mint pill.
Same Tailwind class covers hover-brightness and active-scale.

scroll_reveal_options.rationale: Use framer-motion `whileInView` with `viewport={{ once: true, amount: 0.3 }}` so the card animates once when ~30% is visible, not every scroll.

implementation_notes: CourseCard must be a Client Component ('use client') because of framer-motion whileInView + stagger.

Wrap root in motion.div (or motion.article) with `variants` + `initial='hidden' whileInView='visible'`. Each child uses the same variants object — framer-motion resolves stagger via the parent's transition.
Total stagger budget: image(0) → title(60) → body(120) → feature-row/price(180) → cta(240) → caption(300) = 300ms span, within the ≤300ms requirement.
Hover-lift is Tailwind-only — does NOT conflict with the motion.div mount animation; framer-motion's `animate` target only sets opacity+y, leaving CSS transitions free for transform on hover.

If the whole card is wrapped in next/link (clickable surface), use `<Link>` as the motion component via `motion(Link)` OR place the Link as an absolutely-positioned overlay with a sibling `<button>` for the CTA. Recommended: nested Links with `onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}` on the CTA, OR (cleaner) keep only the CTA as the navigation target and make the card itself non-clickable. Orchestrator should pick.

Respect prefers-reduced-motion: framer-motion auto-handles when MotionConfig reducedMotion='user' is set at app root; otherwise wrap CourseCard with `<MotionConfig reducedMotion='user'>` or branch on useReducedMotion().

components.notes: Purely decorative. No individual entrance motion — covered by the parent CourseCard's `feature-row` fade-in-up (the row as a single target, not per-tile). No hover, no click. Renders as a static server-component-safe div with icon + label.

motion.rationale: Subtle press-scale + brightness shift on hover/active. If the existing Button primitive already ships hover/press states, reuse them; only add these classes if the mint variant lacks them.

actions.rationale: Label 'Book spot now' → course-specific booking page. See unresolved list — same destination as CourseCard.cta action above.

components.notes: Existing primitive — codebase-scout to confirm Button has (or can grow) a `mint` variant. Motion + action are wired by CourseCard; this entry documents the contract so the builder doesn't double-animate.

global_notes: Bodhi tone is calm/yoga — keep durations 300–400ms, translates ≤12px, no spring bounces.
Only framer-motion usage on this card is the entrance stagger + fade-in-up. Hover/press states are Tailwind-only to keep the bundle thin.

If this CourseCard is rendered in a list of multiple courses, the parent list should NOT add an outer stagger — each card already stagger-reveals its own children. Outer-list stagger would compound and feel slow.

unresolved.reason: No prototype link in Figma. Slug derived from the course title. Confirm with user whether (a) Bodhi uses /courses/[slug] routing, (b) the entire card is clickable vs CTA-only, and (c) the canonical slug ('decoding-what-is-prana' vs 'what-is-prana' vs an ID).

No prototype link. 'Book spot now' implies a booking page. Two plausible patterns: nested `/courses/[slug]/book` (REST-ish) or query-param `/book?course=decoding-what-is-prana` (flat checkout). Confirm with user.
Same destination as CourseCard.cta — depends on the routing decision above.

unresolved.guess: CTA-only is clickable; the card surface is non-interactive
unresolved.reason: If both the card and the nested CTA navigate, React DOM nests <a> inside <a> which is invalid. Builder must pick one. Recommended default: CTA-only nav, card stays decorative (drop the root navigate action). Orchestrator should confirm before build.
